MINT transforms this into a near-automated, zero-noise transition.

What MINT Does: Five Core Capabilities

  • Discovery & Assessment : MINT automatically scans the current CPI Neo environment and builds a complete inventory of iFlows, connections, and dependencies. This provides a clear baseline of what exists today and precisely what needs attention for the migration, reducing the discovery effort by up to 40-50% compared to a manual assessment exercise.
  • Migration Blueprint : MINT produces structured compatibility reports that identify which artefacts are ready for direct migration to BTP CF and which require adjustment. This blueprint eliminates guesswork from the programme plan and ensures that effort estimates are grounded in a genuine analysis of the landscape rather than informed assumptions.
  • One-Click Migration Accelerators : This is where MINT’s 95% automation factor is realised. Built-in migration accelerators automate the conversion of iFlows and configurations that would otherwise require days of manual rework per interface. The migration effort that would traditionally occupy a team of consultants for months is compressed into a structured, automated execution, drastically cutting project timelines and resource requirements while preserving integration logic fidelity.
  • Event Mesh Enablement : MINT does not just replicate the Neo landscape on Cloud Foundry, it opens the door to future-ready architecture. Migration paths supported by MINT include enablement for SAP Event Mesh and event-driven integration models, allowing organisations to modernise not just their runtime environment but their integration architecture patterns, moving from point-to-point towards scalable, event-driven designs that are responsive to real-time business events across the enterprise.
  • Progress Tracking Dashboard : A visual dashboard provides end-to-end visibility into migration progress, blockers, and success metrics throughout the programme. Stakeholders and programme managers can track milestones and monitor completion rates in real time, ensuring the project stays on schedule with complete transparency, giving leadership confidence at every stage of the transition.

What the Numbers Say

MINT delivers up to 95% automation for Neo-to-Cloud Foundry migrations, the highest automation factor across the entire iVolve portfolio. Discovery effort is reduced by 40-50%, migration effort by up to 75%, and post-migration operational costs by up to 30%. The result is a zero-noise transition: interfaces move, the business keeps running, and the integration team emerges with a modern, cloud-native landscape on SAP BTP Integration Suite.

 

With the Neo-to-CF migration complete and the wider landscape modernisation underway, the next critical phase is ensuring that every migrated interface works exactly as intended. That is the job of the TestEase Bundle.

Phase 3: TestEase : Automated Testing as a First-Class Citizen

Testing is where migration projects historically consume disproportionate time and cost. Creating test cases, preparing test payloads, executing tests, analysing results, and retesting after fixes; all of this was manual, slow, and dependent on business involvement at inconvenient times.

 The TestEase Bundle automates this across multiple testing dimensions:

  • Functional Testing : TestEase automatically generates test suites, test cases, and payloads from SAP business documents. End-to-end message flow validation traces both technical and functional acknowledgments. Tests are reusable, payloads are captured at each lifecycle event and can be replayed for regression testing.
  • B2B / EDI Testing : Trading partner configuration testing validates sender and receiver ID mapping, message types, document standards, and partner-specific transformations. Real-time B2B execution monitoring provides step-by-step traceability for every event and transformation.
  • Test Coverage Analysis : The TestCoverage feature within TestEase provides enterprise-grade test coverage analysis with intelligent path detection and visualisation, giving teams confidence that the migrated landscape has been comprehensively validated, not just spot-checked.

 

iVolve vs. SAP's Native Tool: Unlike SAP's native migration tool, iVolve supports both Lift & Shift and Customisation, includes integrated automated testing, supports non-SAP middleware discovery, and generates migration approach documentation and delivers many more capabilities, all as standard.

 

The Compound Effect: Faster, Cheaper, More Reliable

The combined effect of automated discovery, automated migration, and automated testing is not merely additive - it is multiplicative. Programmes that would have taken 18 months complete in 8. Migrations that would have required 30 consultants require 10. Test phases that would have consumed 3 months complete in 3 weeks.

Tarento's successful programmes demonstrates predictability and consistency. These are not exceptional outcomes; they are the reliable result of applying the iVolve automation framework at scale.

 In the next blog of this series, we move beyond the migration itself to examine how iVolve's OpEase Suite ensures the post-live landscape operates with the resilience, governance, and intelligence that a modern enterprise integration platform demands.

 

Eager to Get Started: Ready to see the automation in action? Tarento offers a Discovery and Migration Pilot engagement to demonstrate iVolve's automation capability against a sample of your actual interfaces before you committing to a full programme.
Lets connect. Drop me a note on shabarish.nair@tarento.com.

entry.title: iVolve Series | Part D : Beyond Go-Live
entry.content:
Note: This blog is a continuation of The iVolve Series | Your Integration Estate Is Holding You Back. Well, It Doesn’t Have To. Read iVolve Series | Part C: Automate Everything before continuing.

Go-live is not the end of an integration modernisation programme. It is the beginning of a new operating model. For many organisations, the transition from legacy middleware to SAP Integration Suite on BTP represents the first time their integration landscape has been managed with cloud-native DevOps practices i.e. continuous deployment, automated governance, proactive monitoring, and genuine high availability.

The challenge is that SAP Integration Suite, while a powerful platform, does not come with enterprise-grade DevOps tooling out of the box. Organisations that invest in a migration without investing in the operational layer often find themselves replicating the operational debt of the legacy landscape on a newer platform.

Tarento's OpEase Suite was built to close this gap. This blog examines the four key components and how they work together to deliver a resilient, well-governed, AI-enhanced integration operations environment.

 

OneFailover : Near-Zero Downtime for Critical Landscapes

For large integration landscapes handling business-critical transactions, planned and unplanned downtime is not just an IT inconvenience; it is a business risk with measurable financial impact. SAP Integration Suite maintenance windows, regional incidents, or platform upgrades can require traffic to be rerouted to a standby environment. Without tooling, this is a manual, error-prone process that takes hours and relies on precise coordination across multiple teams.

OneFailover provides an operational layer that standardises and automates this process entirely.

How OneFailover Works

OneFailover continuously maintains a failover tenant or runtime in a 'ready state' fully synchronised with the primary environment, tested, and prepared for immediate cutover. The core capabilities span several dimensions:

  • Sync & Orchestration: Full-scope synchronisation of iFlows, Message Mappings, Value Mappings, and Scripts across tenants, with dependency-aware ordering and group/sub-group priorities
  • Failover Control: Group-based methodology with deployment prioritisation and manual listener cutover, using OneFailover Profiles to map primary-to-failover configurations
  • Backup & Restore: GitHub-based, artefact-level versioning with selective restore capability and overwrite guardrails
  • Runtime Awareness: Edge Cell and multi-runtime/tenant awareness, with a persistence layer to inventory iFlows and tenant metadata
  • Observability: Operational Status and Monitoring dashboards, Readiness scores, activity and execution logs, and throughput optimisations
  • Security: User Credentials, Global Variables, Number Ranges, and Access Policies included in sync and backup, with secret-safe metadata handling

     

OneFailover integrates with enterprise routing patterns including Azure Traffic Manager, internal DNS solutions like F5 or Citrix NetScaler, and GitHub for version control, fitting naturally into existing enterprise infrastructure.

 

When to Use OneFailover

OneFailover is relevant across four operational scenarios: planned maintenance windows where manual coordination effort needs to be eliminated; regional resiliency programmes where a failover posture for critical interfaces must be maintained continuously; peak events where failover needs to be pre-staged for traffic surges; and compliance and audit requirements where evidence of DR readiness and traceable execution history is needed.

 

iFlow AI Advisory App : Design Intelligence for Dev Ease

The iFlow AI Advisory App addresses a challenge that emerges as integration teams start developing new flows and extending migrated ones on SAP Integration Suite: without deep platform expertise, developers default to familiar patterns from legacy platforms that are not optimal on BTP.

The iFlow AI Advisory App provides automated design recommendations, guiding integration teams to the most appropriate iFlow patterns for each use case. Rather than waiting for an architecture review to flag suboptimal designs, the advisory capability operates at development time, preventing technical debt from accumulating in the new landscape before it has even started.

This capability is particularly valuable for organisations that are extending their SAP Integration Suite footprint beyond the migrated interfaces into net-new integration development, ensuring that the same automation-first, pattern-led approach that characterised the migration carries through into ongoing development.

 

Transport Management & Governance Tool

Governance of the integration landscape, managing the lifecycle of iFlows from development through QA to production, is a critical operational capability that is easy to underestimate. Without structured transport management, teams resort to manual deployment procedures, version confusion, and the risk of deploying untested changes to production.

The iVolve Transport Management and Governance Tool provides the operational rigour that enterprise integration landscapes require:

  • Full transport management capabilities with controlled promotion from development through QA to production
  • Artefact comparison and version management -> compare versions before deployment to understand exactly what is changing
  • Easy access to adapter configuration details across the landscape
  • Externalised parameter management for environment-specific configurations

 

Enhanced Monitoring and AI-Powered Operations

SAP Integration Suite's native monitoring provides a solid baseline, but enterprise operations teams consistently find they need capabilities that go further particularly around error management, reporting, and proactive intelligence.

Enhanced Monitoring Tool

Built on SAP standards for seamless integration, the Enhanced Monitoring Tool provides full monitoring reports with downloadable error outputs for efficient debugging. Its restart functionality and searchable interface  by sender, receiver, message type, application ID, date, time, and statistical aggregates, enables operations teams to move from reactive firefighting to proactive management.

AI-Powered Alert Notifications

iVolve's AI-enhanced notification capability leverages SAP BTP's Integration Suite AI Adapter to enrich alert emails with GenAI-generated recommendations, giving operations teams not just the notification of a failure, but context-aware guidance on likely causes and resolution steps. This is the beginning of a shift from monitoring to intelligence-assisted operations.

Periodic Monitoring AI Agent

Looking ahead to the 2026 roadmap, Tarento is building out an autonomous Periodic Monitoring Agent that will automate routine manual monitoring activities using the iVolve AI platform, removing the operational overhead of scheduled checks and exception reviews from the integration operations team entirely.

 

The OpEase Suite as a Competitive Advantage

 Organisations that deploy the full OpEase Suite alongside their SAP Integration Suite migration achieve something qualitatively different from those that treat DevOps as an afterthought. They have a resilient, governed, intelligence-enhanced integration operations model from day one of go-live. Their operations teams are not firefighting  but are managing by exception, with tooling that surfaces the information they need and automates the responses they would otherwise need to execute manually.

 

 

Bringing It All Together: The Full iVolve Journey

Across this series, we have traced the complete arc of integration modernisation with iVolve; from the strategic imperative and business case, through automated discovery, migration and testing, to the operational excellence of the OpEase Suite. In the next and final blog of this series, we will deep dive into OneFailOver Application and how it helps enterprises architect and manage high-resiliency, and take control over the HA/Failover process in their organisations.

Across the series, the consistent thread has been automation: reducing the manual effort, the human error, and the programme risk that have historically made integration modernisation programmes difficult to justify and difficult to deliver.

Tarento’s iVolve programme is the product of deep SAP integration expertise, proprietary tooling built and refined over dozens of programmes, and a genuine commitment to delivering the ROI that customers calculate before they commit. 

 

Take the Next Step: iVolve is available on the SAP Store. Tarento is an SAP Validated Partner Use Case (VPUC) holder and SAP Migration Factory Partner. To start a Discovery engagement or run an ROI analysis for your landscape, drop me a note on shabarish.nair@tarento.com.

entry.title: iVolve Series | Part E : When Downtime Is Not an Option
entry.content:
Note: This blog is part of The iVolve Series | Your Integration Estate Is Holding You Back. Well, It Doesn’t Have To. Read the previous blog in this series, iVolve Series | Part D : Beyond Go-Live before continuing.

It is 11:47 PM. An online retailer’s Black Friday weekend promotion has just gone live. Orders are flooding in at three times the normal rate. Every order triggers a cascade of integration events; stock reservation, payment confirmation, warehouse dispatch, carrier booking, customer notification. All of this runs through SAP Integration Suite. And at 11:52 PM, comes an announcement of an emergency platform maintenance window. The primary tenant will be unavailable for the next 45 minutes.

For organisations without a failover strategy, this is a crisis. For organisations running OneFailover, it is a non-event. Traffic is rerouted to the failover tenant in seconds. The integration landscape keeps running. And the end customers never notice. Seamless as seamless can be!

This blog examines why integration resilience is a board-level concern for any enterprise that relies on SAP Integration Suite for real-time business operations and how Tarento’s OneFailover application transforms a complex, manual, high-risk process into a repeatable, automated, and auditable programme capability.

 

The Integration Layer Is the Business

For a previous generation of enterprises, integration middleware was infrastructure; important, but not business-critical in the way that customer-facing systems were. That distinction has collapsed. For any organisation that has consolidated its integration estate onto SAP Integration Suite, the platform is now the nervous system of real-time business operations.

Consider the industries where this is most acute. A large e-commerce retailer processes tens of thousands of orders per hour during peak periods; every order is an integration event. A global manufacturer runs real-time production planning and supplier collaboration through integration flows; a 30-minute outage ripples into production delays, penalty clauses, and customer escalations. A financial services firm processes payment instructions and regulatory reporting through integration; downtime is a compliance exposure, not just an operational inconvenience. A healthcare provider coordinates patient data flows, bed management, and pharmacy dispensing through its integration platform; unavailability can directly affect patient outcomes.

For all of these organisations, the question of what happens when SAP Integration Suite is unavailable - whether for a planned maintenance window, a regional incident, or a capacity event is not a theoretical IT risk. It is a live business continuity question that demands a credible, tested answer.

 

The Downtime Problem Is More Complex Than It Looks

SAP Integration Suite is a managed cloud service. This brings enormous benefits - Continuous updates, elastic scaling, no infrastructure management overhead. But it also means that maintenance is scheduled by SAP, not by the enterprise. Planned maintenance windows are notified in advance but are not negotiable. And unplanned incidents, while rare on a well-operated cloud platform, cannot be eliminated entirely.

The naïve response is to simply provision a second tenant and manually copy everything across when needed. In practice, this approach fails in three ways.

First, the synchronisation problem: a large integration landscape may contain hundreds of iFlows, message mappings, value mappings, scripts, security artefacts, and configuration parameters. Keeping a secondary tenant in sync with all of these manually is a significant ongoing operational burden, one that is frequently deprioritised until it is too late.

Second, the execution problem: when an incident occurs or a maintenance window opens, switching traffic to a secondary tenant requires precise, sequenced steps across multiple systems - DNS or traffic manager changes, iFlow activation, listener cutover, and verification. Doing this under pressure, manually, from a runbook, at 2 AM, creates unacceptable risk of human error.

Third, the governance problem: many regulated industries require documented, auditable evidence of disaster recovery capability. A manual failover process that lives in a shared document and has never been tested in a controlled way does not satisfy this requirement.

 

The Three Failure Modes of Manual Failover

Synchronisation drift means the failover tenant is never truly ready. Execution risk means the switchover itself is the most dangerous moment. Governance gaps mean the organisation cannot demonstrate readiness to auditors or regulators. OneFailover addresses all three simultaneously.

 

 

Introducing OneFailover: An Operational Layer for Integration Resilience

OneFailover is Tarento’s purpose-built application for SAP Integration Suite tenant resilience — available on the SAP Store and used by enterprises running large, mission-critical integration landscapes. It was formerly known as Unified Failover Management and has evolved into a comprehensive operational platform that addresses every dimension of the failover challenge: continuous synchronisation, controlled cutover, backup and recovery, observability, and governance.

The core design principle is that failover should never be an emergency response. It should be a routine, well-rehearsed capability that an operations team can invoke with confidence, whether for a scheduled maintenance window, a regional resiliency exercise, or an actual incident. OneFailover is built around that principle from the ground up.

 

The Six Pillars of OneFailover

 Pillar 1: Continuous Sync and Orchestration

OneFailover maintains a full-scope, continuously synchronised replica of the primary tenant across all artefact types: iFlows, Message Mappings, Value Mappings, and Scripts. Synchronisation is dependency-aware, artefacts that depend on each other are always moved together, in the correct sequence, preventing partial deployments that would cause failures. Group and sub-group priorities allow integration teams to define which business domains and interface clusters are synchronised first, ensuring the most critical processes are always in the highest state of readiness on the failover tenant. Single-side or both-side deployment options give teams flexibility in how they manage the synchronisation cadence.

Pillar 2: Failover Control and Cutover Execution

When the moment of cutover arrives, planned or unplanned, OneFailover provides deterministic, orchestrated execution. The group-based methodology ensures that interface clusters are activated on the failover tenant in the correct business-aware sequence: order management before warehouse execution, payment before fulfilment. OneFailover Profiles capture the complete primary-to-failover mapping configuration, including Git targets, so that the same cutover procedure can be repeated identically every time - in a drill, in a maintenance window, or in a genuine incident. An admin lock prevents simultaneous actions by multiple operators during a cutover, eliminating the risk of conflicting changes in a high-pressure situation. Manual listener cutover ensures that human confirmation is required at the point of traffic switching, maintaining control at the most consequential moment.

The cutover integrates with enterprise routing infrastructure. For inbound traffic switching, OneFailover works with Azure Traffic Manager in a priority-based setup, or with internal DNS solutions such as F5 or Citrix NetScaler, fitting into the network architecture that the enterprise already operates rather than requiring new infrastructure to be introduced.

Pillar 3: Backup, Restore and Version Control

OneFailover provides GitHub-based, artefact-level versioning for the integration landscape. Every iFlow, mapping, and configuration is versioned individually, enabling selective restore with overwrite guardrails that prevent accidental rollbacks. Failover configurations, the OneFailover Profiles, can be exported and imported, enabling them to be maintained, version-controlled, and shared across teams. This creates a complete audit trail of the integration landscape’s state over time, supporting change management, incident investigation, and regulatory compliance requirements.

Pillar 4: Multi-Runtime and Multi-Tenant Awareness

Large enterprise integration landscapes are not homogeneous. They typically span multiple SAP Integration Suite tenants, multiple runtimes including Cloud Foundry and Edge Integration Cells for sensitive on-premise data, and multiple business domains with different criticality levels and ownership. OneFailover is designed for this complexity. Its persistence layer maintains a complete inventory of iFlows and tenant metadata across the full landscape, providing a single point of control and visibility regardless of how many tenants and runtimes are in scope. Edge Cell awareness means that even hybrid landscapes  with interfaces running on-premise for data sovereignty or latency reasons, are fully included in the failover posture.

Pillar 5: Observability and Operational Dashboards

Knowing that a failover tenant exists is not the same as knowing that it is ready. OneFailover provides continuous Readiness Scores for each tenant and runtime, a quantified measure of how current the failover environment is relative to the primary, how recently it was tested, and whether any synchronisation gaps or configuration discrepancies exist. Operational Status and Monitoring dashboards give integration operations teams real-time visibility into the state of both primary and failover environments, with activity and execution logs that provide a complete record of every action taken. Throughput optimisations ensure that synchronisation activity does not create performance overhead on the primary tenant during business hours.

Pillar 6: Security, Compliance and Secret Safety

Integration landscapes contain sensitive security artefacts: User Credentials, Global Variables, Number Ranges, and Access Policies. OneFailover includes all of these in its synchronisation and backup scope, ensuring that the failover tenant is not just functionally complete but operationally ready to process real transactions. Security is maintained through SAP BTP Identity Authentication Service-powered authentication and strict secret-safe metadata handling: credentials and sensitive values are referenced by hash or reference only, never stored in plain text in version control. Strong guardrails prevent accidental exposure of production credentials during failover operations.

 

Why SAP’s Standard Capabilities Are Not Enough: The Case for OneFailover

A common assumption when enterprises first deploy SAP Integration Suite is that high availability and disaster recovery are simply included, that because SAP is a managed cloud service, the resilience problem is solved by default. This assumption is partially correct and significantly incomplete. SAP Integration Suite does provide genuine, well-engineered high availability within a single region through multi-availability zone redundancy.

For most routine operational scenarios, the above is sufficient. But for enterprises that require cross-regional disaster recovery, planned maintenance execution without service interruption, automated failover with deterministic cutover sequences, and the kind of audit-ready, governance-controlled resilience posture that regulated industries must demonstrate, SAP’s native capabilities leave substantial gaps that OneFailover is specifically engineered to fill.

The table below maps each dimension of enterprise integration resilience against what SAP provides natively and what OneFailover adds, making explicit where the standard platform ends and where OneFailover begins.

The pattern across every dimension in this table is consistent: SAP provides the foundation, and OneFailover provides the operational layer that transforms that foundation into a fully governed, automated, and enterprise-grade resilience posture. These are capabilities that matter most when a regional incident, a regulatory audit, or a peak trading event puts real pressure on the integration landscape. Treating them as optional is, in effect, a decision to accept that risk rather than manage it.

For integration teams making the case internally for OneFailover investment, this table is often the most effective starting point: it places SAP’s own standard offering alongside OneFailover’s capabilities, making the gap visible and the value proposition concrete across every dimension that leadership, operations, and compliance stakeholders care about.

 

Planning for the Four Scenarios That Matter

OneFailover’s capabilities map directly onto the four operational scenarios where integration resilience is genuinely tested:

Scenario 1: Planned Maintenance Windows

SAP notifies maintenance windows in advance, giving integration teams time to prepare. With OneFailover, preparation is straightforward: verify the Readiness Score, run a pre-cutover sync to ensure the failover tenant reflects all recent changes, and execute the controlled cutover through the OneFailover portal. The maintenance window passes with no business disruption. When the primary tenant is restored, failback follows the same orchestrated process in reverse. What would otherwise require a team of engineers working through a complex runbook becomes a two-person operation managed through an intuitive interface.

Scenario 2: Regional Resiliency and Unplanned Incidents

For organisations with a global integration landscape, regional incidents like a cloud availability zone event, a network disruption, a regional regulatory action etc may require rerouting integration traffic to a different data centre or availability zone. The risk calculus has also shifted. Recent geopolitical conflicts most notably the US/Israel and Iran confrontation have seen data centres targeted as part of military operations, causing prolonged regional outages that no amount of within-region redundancy could mitigate. Cloud infrastructure is not insulated from the consequences of armed conflict, and organisations with dependencies in or routing through affected regions are finding that out in real time. Regional resilience is no longer a contingency for rare technical failures it is a standing operational requirement. 

OneFailover’s multi-tenant and multi-runtime awareness, combined with its integration with Azure Traffic Manager and enterprise DNS infrastructure, makes regional rerouting a controlled operation rather than a crisis response. The failover tenant in a secondary region is continuously maintained in a ready state so that when rerouting is needed, whether triggered by a scheduled maintenance window or an unplanned regional event, the tenant is already current and the cutover can be executed without scrambling to synchronise artefacts under pressure.

Scenario 3: Peak Traffic Events

For e-commerce retailers, peak trading periods i.e. Black Friday, Cyber Monday, end-of-season sales etc represent the highest-value and highest-risk windows of the year. An integration outage during a 48-hour peak event can represent millions in lost revenue and lasting reputational damage. OneFailover provides the assurance that integration teams need to approach peak periods with confidence: a fully synchronised, tested, and ready failover tenant that can absorb traffic within seconds if the primary environment comes under strain or experiences an incident. The Readiness Score dashboard gives leadership a quantified, real-time view of DR preparedness in the days and hours before a peak event goes live.

Scenario 4: Compliance, Audit and DR Certification

Regulated industries; financial services, healthcare, utilities, public sector are increasingly required to demonstrate integration resilience as part of broader IT operational resilience or business continuity frameworks. The EU’s Digital Operational Resilience Act (DORA), for example, requires financial entities to test their ICT continuity measures, maintain documented recovery procedures, and demonstrate that critical third-party dependencies, including cloud platforms are covered by their resilience programmes.

OneFailover’s activity logs, execution records, Readiness Scores, and repeatable playbooks provide exactly the evidence base that internal audit, external auditors, and regulators need to see: documented DR capability, tested regularly, with traceable execution history.

 

What Enterprises Gain: The Business Value Equation

 OneFailover delivers value across four dimensions that map directly to business outcomes rather than IT metrics:

  • Lower operational risk: integration outages during maintenance windows, peak events, or incidents are eliminated as a source of business disruption. The risk is not mitigated but is engineered out of the operating model
  • Faster, repeatable failover: what previously took hours of manual coordination is reduced to minutes of portal-guided execution. The same process runs identically every time, removing the dependency on tribal knowledge and individual expertise
  • Improved governance: every action is logged, every artefact is versioned, and every failover execution is traceable. The integration landscape becomes auditable in a way it never was with legacy middleware or manual processes
  • Higher confidence: integration teams, operations leadership, and the business can approach maintenance windows and peak events knowing that the failover posture is quantified, tested, and ready

 

 The 2026 Roadmap: Making OneFailover Even Smarter

Tarento’s 2026 roadmap for OneFailover extends the platform into several high-value adjacent areas. API Management integration will bring API policies and configurations into the sync and failover scope, ensuring that API-fronted integrations maintain consistent behaviour across primary and failover environments. A Centralised Key Vault integration will further strengthen the security model for credential and secret management across tenants. Advanced Proactive Alerting will shift OneFailover from a reactive tool into a predictive one, notifying operations teams of synchronisation drift or readiness degradation before it becomes a risk. Trading Partner Management capabilities will extend the failover posture to cover B2B integration configurations, ensuring that EDI and partner-facing integrations are included in the resilience framework alongside internal A2A flows.

 

Integration Resilience as a Programme Capability, Not an Afterthought

The organisations that get the most from SAP Integration Suite are those that treat it as a strategic platform, not just infrastructure. That means investing not only in migration quality and development standards, but in the operational model that keeps the platform running at the level of reliability the business requires.

For any enterprise where integration is the backbone of real-time business operations, whether in retail, manufacturing, financial services, healthcare, or logistics - OneFailover is not an optional enhancement to a well-functioning SAP Integration Suite deployment. It is the mechanism that makes a well-functioning deployment genuinely enterprise-ready: predictable, resilient, governed, and prepared for the scenarios that matter most.

The scenario at the start of this blog; an e-retailer navigating a Friday night urgent maintenance window at peak trading, illustrates what is at stake. With OneFailover, the answer to that scenario is simple: the integration landscape keeps running. The customers keep doing what they do. The business keeps moving. And when the primary tenant comes back online, failback runs just as cleanly as failover did.

 

Get Started: OneFailover is available on the SAP Store. To see a live demonstration of failover execution and the Readiness Score dashboard, or to discuss how OneFailover fits into your SAP Integration Suite operating model, drop me a note on shabarish.nair@tarento.com.
